language.title: Language Settings
language.uiLanguageDesc: The language used for the UI interface.
language.preferredLanguageDesc: The language that Codee should use for communication.

other.title: Other Settings
other.customInstructionsDesc: These instructions are added to the end of the system prompt sent with every request.
other.planActSeparateModels: Use different models for Plan and Act modes
other.planActSeparateModelsDesc: Switching between Plan and Act mode will persist the API and model used in the previous mode. This may be helpful e.g. when using a strong reasoning model to architect a plan for a cheaper coding model to act on.
other.resetStateDesc: This will reset all global state and secret storage in the extension.

feedback.text: If you have any questions or feedback, feel free to open an issue at

features.enableCheckpointsDesc: Enables extension to save checkpoints of workspace throughout the task. Uses git under the hood which may not work well with large workspaces.
features.enableMcpMarketplaceDesc: Enables the MCP Marketplace tab for discovering and installing MCP servers.
features.mcpDisplayModeDesc: Controls how MCP responses are displayed: plain text, rich formatting with links/images, or markdown rendering.
features.collapseMcpResponsesDesc: Sets the default display mode for MCP response panels
features.openaiReasoningEffortDesc: Reasoning effort for the OpenAI family of models(applies to all OpenAI model providers)
features.enableStrictPlanModeDesc: Enforces strict tool use while in plan mode, preventing file edits.
features.enableFocusChainDesc: Enables enhanced task progress tracking and automatic focus chain list management throughout tasks.
features.focusChainRemindIntervalDesc: Interval (in messages) to remind Codee about its focus chain checklist (1-100). Lower values provide more frequent reminders.
features.enableAutoCondenseDesc: Enables advanced context management system which uses LLM based condensing for next-gen models.
features.enableSkillsDesc:
Skills file location:
Windows:
Global: C:\Users\xxxx\.codee\skills
Project: Current open directory\.codee\skills
MacOS:
Global: /Users/xxxx/.codee/skills
Project: Current open directory/.codee/skills

features.nativeToolCallDesc: Use native function calling when available
features.strictPlanModeDesc: Prevents file edits while in Plan mode
features.backgroundEditDesc: Allow edits without stealing editor focus
features.checkpointsDesc: Save progress at key points for easy rollback
features.clineWebToolsDesc: Access web browsing and search capabilities
features.worktreesDesc: Enables git worktree management for running parallel Cline tasks
features.yoloModeDesc: Execute tasks without user's confirmation. Auto-switches from Plan to Act mode and disables the ask question tool. Use with extreme caution
features.remoteConfigTooltip: This setting is managed by your organization's remote configuration
features.openaiReasoningEffortDesc: Control the depth of reasoning for OpenAI o-series models
features.mcpDisplayModeDesc: Controls how MCP responses are displayed

terminal.defaultProfileDesc: Select the default terminal Codee will use. 'Default' uses your VSCode global setting.
terminal.shellTimeoutDesc: Set how long Codee waits for shell integration to activate before executing commands. Increase this value if you experience terminal connection timeouts.
terminal.aggressiveReuseDesc: When enabled, Codee will reuse existing terminal windows that aren't in the current working directory. Disable this if you experience issues with task lockout after a terminal command.
terminal.executionModeDesc: Choose whether Codee runs commands in the VS Code terminal or a background process.

browser.disableToolUsageDesc: Prevent Codee from using browser actions (e.g. launch, click, type).
browser.viewportSizeDesc: Set the size of the browser viewport for screenshots and interactions.
browser.remoteConnectionDesc: Enable Codee to use your Chrome{{chromePath}}. You can specify a custom path below. Using a remote browser connection requires starting Chrome in debug mode{{debugMode}}
browser.manualDebug: manually (--remote-debugging-port=9222) or using the button below. Enter the host address or leave it blank for automatic discovery.
browser.customArgsDesc: Space-separated arguments to pass to the browser executable.

debug.resetDesc: This will reset all global state and secret storage in the extension.

api.complexPrompts: Codee uses complex prompts and works best with Claude or deepseek-v3 models. Less capable models may not work as expected.
api.keyStoredLocally: This key is stored locally and only used to make API requests from this extension.
api.defaultBehaviorDesc: Default behavior is to load balance requests across providers (like AWS, Google Vertex, Anthropic), prioritizing price while considering provider uptime
api.priceProviderDesc: Sort providers by price, prioritizing the lowest cost provider
api.throughputProviderDesc: Sort providers by throughput, prioritizing the provider with the highest throughput (may increase cost)
api.latencyProviderDesc: Sort providers by response time, prioritizing the provider with the lowest latency
api.oauthPathDesc: Path to the OAuth credentials file. Leave empty to use the default location (~/.gemini/oauth_creds.json).
api.projectIdAutoDesc: This project ID was automatically discovered from your OAuth credentials.
api.geminiCliDesc: This provider uses OAuth authentication from the Gemini CLI tool and does not require API keys. If you haven't authenticated yet, please run
api.geminiCliRequirements:
• First, you need to install the **Gemini CLI tool**
• Then, run **gemini** in your terminal and make sure you **Log in with Google**
• Only works with **personal Google accounts** (not Google Workspace accounts)
• Does not use API keys - authentication is handled via OAuth
• Requires the Gemini CLI tool to be installed and authenticated first
api.freeTierAccess: ✓ Free tier access via OAuth authentication
api.complexPromptsNote: Note: Codee uses complex prompts and works best with Claude models. Less capable models may not work as expected.

chat.planModeTooltip: In Plan mode, Codee will gather information to architect a plan
chat.actModeTooltip: In Act mode, Codee will complete the task immediately

welcome.title: Hi, I'm Codee
welcome.description: You need to provide me with a detailed software development requirement description, so that I can break down the requirements into multiple steps for execution. I can view files, modify files, write files, execute commands, etc. I can even use MCP to create new tools and expand my abilities.
welcome.signupText: Sign up for an account to get started for free, or use an API key that provides access to models like Claude 3.7 Sonnet.

autoApprove.title: Auto-approve
autoApprove.description: Auto-approve allows Codee to perform the following actions without asking for permission. Please use with caution and only enable if you understand the risks.
autoApprove.maxRequestsDesc: Codee will automatically make this many API requests before asking for approval to proceed with the task.
autoApprove.notificationsDesc: Receive system notifications when Codee requires approval to proceed or when a task is completed.

enableAutoApprove.label: Enable auto-approve
enableAutoApprove.description: Toggle the auto-approve feature on or off.

readFiles.label: Read project files
readFiles.description: Allows Codee to read files within your workspace.

readFilesExternally.label: Read all files
readFilesExternally.description: Allows Codee to read any file on your computer.

editFiles.label: Edit project files
editFiles.description: Allows Codee to modify files within your workspace.

editFilesExternally.label: Edit all files
editFilesExternally.description: Allows Codee to modify any file on your computer.

executeSafeCommands.label: Execute safe commands
executeSafeCommands.description: Allows Codee to execute of safe terminal commands. If the model determines a command is potentially destructive, it will still require approval.

executeAllCommands.label: Execute all commands
executeAllCommands.description: Allows Codee to execute all terminal commands. Use at your own risk.

useBrowser.label: Use the browser
useBrowser.description: Allows Codee to launch and interact with any website in a browser.

useMcp.label: Use MCP servers
useMcp.description: Allows Codee to use configured MCP servers which may modify filesystem or interact with APIs.

enableNotifications.label: Enable notifications
enableNotifications.description: Receive system notifications when Codee requires approval to proceed or when a task is completed.

memorybank.title: Memory Bank
memorybank.description: The Memory Bank is a structured documentation system that allows Codee to maintain context across sessions. It transforms Codee from a stateless assistant into a persistent development partner that can effectively "remember" your project details over time.

codeIndex.title: Code Index
codeIndex.description: Configure codebase indexing settings to enable semantic search of your project.
codeIndex.disabled: Code index disabled, please open it to use
codeIndex.downloadError: Download error, please download manually and place in the specified location,
codeIndex.no_workspace: No workspace open - please open a workspace folder to use code indexing

addServer.title: Add a remote MCP server by providing a name and its URL endpoint. Learn more
addServer.connectingMessage: Connecting to server... This may take a few seconds.

installed.useCommunityMadeServers: enables communication with locally running MCP servers that provide additional tools and resources to extend Codee's capabilities. You can use
installed.createNewMcpServer: or ask Codee to create new tools specific to your workflow (e.g., "add a tool that gets the latest npm docs").

account.title: Account
account.signUpMessage: Sign up for an account to get access to the latest models, billing dashboard to view usage and credits, and more upcoming features.
account.agreementPart1: By continuing, you agree to the

CodeeRules.invalidExtension: Only .md, .txt, or no file extension allowed
CodeeRules.ruleDescription: Rules allow you to provide Codee with system-level guidance. Think of them as a persistent way to include context and preferences for your projects or globally for every conversation.
CodeeRules.workflowsDescription: Workflows allow you to define a series of steps to guide Codee through a repetitive set of tasks, such as deploying a service or submitting a PR. To invoke a workflow, type /workflow-name in the chat.

codebaseSearch.wantsToSearch: Codee wants to search the codebase for {{query}}:
codebaseSearch.wantsToSearchWithPath: Codee wants to search the codebase for {{query}} in {{path}}:
codebaseSearch.indexingFailedNoBlocks: Indexing failed: No code blocks were successfully indexed. This usually indicates an embedder configuration issue.
codebaseSearch.indexingFailedCritical: Indexing failed: No code blocks were successfully indexed despite finding files to process. This indicates a critical embedder failure.
codebaseSearch.indexingRequiresWorkspace: Indexing requires an open workspace folder
